solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int findTheLongestBalancedSubstring(String s) {
        int maxLen = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            int zeros = 0, ones = 0;
            for (int j = i; j < s.length(); j++) {
                if (s.charAt(j) == '0') {
                    zeros++;
                } else {
                    ones++;
                }
                if (zeros == ones) {
                    maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, j - i + 1);
                }
            }
        }
        return maxLen;
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution iterates through all possible substrings of the input string and checks if the number of zeroes is equal to the number of ones. It keeps track of the maximum length of a balanced substring found. The main strength is its simplicity and correctness for small input sizes, but the quadratic time complexity makes it inefficient for large strings.>
